title: psoralea plauta
intro: Psoralea plauta, also known as Indian breadroot or Indian turnip, is a plant species native to North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate the thyroid gland and promote the excretion of iodine. The health benefits of Psoralea plauta include its potential to support thyroid function, improve immune system function, and act as an antibacterial agent. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and powdered, or made into a tea or tincture, and is sometimes used to support thyroid health and immune function.
